edges.description: Loss of ATGL co-activation prevents triacylglycerol hydrolysis, so TG droplets accumulate.
edges.source: ABHD5/CGI-58 Deficiency and Loss of ATGL Co-activation

edges.target: ABHD5/CGI-58 Deficiency and Loss of ATGL Co-activation

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic characterization of Chanarin-Dorfman syndrome patients: first report of large deletions in the ABHD5 gene.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in ABHD5/CGI58 gene are associated with CDS

nodes.description: Biallelic ABHD5 (CGI-58) variants eliminate the co-activator of adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL/PNPLA2). ABHD5 itself lacks hydrolase activity but is required to activate PNPLA-family triacylglycerol lipolysis.
nodes.id: ABHD5/CGI-58 Deficiency and Loss of ATGL Co-activation

evidence.reference_title: ABHD5-A Regulator of Lipid Metabolism Essential for Diverse Cellular Functions.
evidence.snippet:
it functions as a co-activating enzyme of
the patatin-like phospholipase domain-containing (PNPLA) protein family that is
involved in triacylglycerol

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic characterization of Chanarin-Dorfman syndrome patients: first report of large deletions in the ABHD5 gene.
evidence.snippet:
The clinical phenotype involves multiple organs and systems,
including liver, eyes, ears, skeletal muscle and central nervous system (CNS)

nodes.description: Undegraded triacylglycerol accumulates as cytoplasmic lipid droplets in most tissues, including skin, liver, muscle, leukocytes, eye, and CNS, driving the multisystem disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic characterization of Chanarin-Dorfman syndrome patients: first report of large deletions in the ABHD5 gene.
evidence.snippet: an intracellular accumulation of triacylglycerol (TG) droplets in most tissues

nodes.description: Hepatic involvement with steatosis and hepatomegaly from triglyceride storage.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic characterization of Chanarin-Dorfman syndrome patients: first report of large deletions in the ABHD5 gene.
evidence.snippet:
The clinical phenotype involves multiple organs and systems,
including liver, eyes, ears, skeletal muscle and central nervous system (CNS)

nodes.description: Hepatomegaly with hepatic steatosis is a frequent visceral feature.

evidence.snippet:
non-bullous congenital ichthyosiform eritrhoderma (NCIE), hepatomegaly and liver
steatosis

nodes.description: Nonbullous congenital ichthyosiform erythroderma is the presenting cutaneous feature.

evidence.reference_title: Chanarin-Dorfman Syndrome (CDS): A Rare Lipid Metabolism Disorder.
evidence.snippet: CDS patients present with a characteristic dermatological finding, ichthyosis, which is a non-bullous white scaling of the skin

nodes.description: Skeletal muscle involvement with lipid-storage myopathy.

The clinical phenotype involves multiple organs and systems,

nodes.description: Hearing impairment reflects involvement of the ears.
